title: Stelis verbiformis
taxonomy: Stelis verbiformis is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. It is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Stelis is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes most modern orchids. Stelis verbiformis is native to Central and South America, adding to its unique classification within the region's flora. Its classification reflects its distinct characteristics and evolutionary history as an orchid species.

intro: Stelis verbiformis, also known as Verbena-like Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis verbiformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis verbiformis is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are commonly used to soothe skin irritations, calm digestive issues, and support immune function.
